release_notes:
### ShibaClaw v0.2.5 🐾

- **Added**: Native Windows desktop launcher with bundled ShibaClaw.exe, system tray integration, and packaged assets.
- **Changed**: The desktop launcher now defaults to a smoother local experience by disabling WebUI auth unless you explicitly re-enable it.
- **Changed**: WebUI session history now cleans channel prefixes from titles and shows dedicated colored channel badges under each session.
- **Fix**: Windows packaged builds no longer crash on startup when `sys.stderr` is unavailable.
- **Fix**: Cron jobs now start and stop with the gateway, hide routine internal prompts from chat history, and run in background workers so long tasks no longer freeze timers.
upgrade_notes: Update to v0.2.5. Restart any running desktop, web, or gateway process after upgrading so the new Windows launcher, cron lifecycle fixes, and WebUI session metadata changes take effect.

changes.note: Version bumped to 0.2.5 and Windows desktop packaging dependencies were added.
Added the v0.2.5 release notes covering desktop, cron, and WebUI updates.
Documented the native Windows desktop launcher and updated release guidance.
Builds the portable Windows bundle used locally and in GitHub Actions.
Generates the icon set bundled into the Windows desktop executable.
Defines the PyInstaller bundle for the native Windows desktop app.
Adds the packaged desktop entry point used by ShibaClaw.exe.
Implements the native pywebview launcher, tray boot flow, and window lifecycle.
Manages the embedded gateway and WebUI runtime used by desktop mode.
Adds the Windows system tray integration for the native desktop app.
Hardens logging so frozen Windows builds do not crash when stderr is missing.
Wires cron service startup and shutdown into the gateway lifecycle and desktop flow.
Runs cron jobs through background workers so long tasks no longer block timers.
Supports the desktop launcher integration and local auth defaults.
Refreshes session rows with cleaner titles, channel badges, and sidebar polish.
Normalizes session titles by stripping channel prefixes before rendering.
